action.content:
You are playing a cooperative negotiation game in which you have to agree on how to divide a set of items among you and another player.

Rules:
(a) You and the other player are given a set of items. Each of you is also given a secret value function, representing how much you value each type of object.
(b) You exchange messages with the other player to agree on who gets which items. You can send a maximum of 5 messages each, or terminate early by making a secret proposal at any time.
(c) You are each asked to submit a secret proposal indicating the items you want formatted in square brackets as follows: "[Proposal: <number> <object name>, <number> <object name>, <...>]"
(d) If your proposals are complementary, i.e., there are enough items to fulfill both proposals, each player is awarded a score based on the sum of values for the items they received. Otherwise, both of you get zero points.
(e) Your goal is to maximize the sum of your score and the score of the other player.

Let us start.

The set of available items is:

1 thimble, 1 soap, 4 stamps

Your secret value function is:

thimble: 3, soap: 7, stamp: 0

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. To make a secret proposal, use the indicated format. Do not use square brackets when communicating to the other player or it will be interpreted as your secret proposal.
action.label: context

action.content: Hello! I'd like to work together to maximize our combined score. To do this efficiently, we should figure out which items each of us values most. I can tell you that I have no interest in the stamps at all - you're welcome to all 4 of them if you find them valuable. What are your preferences among the items?
action.label: response

Hello! I'd like to work together to maximize our combined score. To do this efficiently, we should figure out which items each of us values most. I can tell you that I have no interest in the stamps at all - you're welcome to all 4 of them if you find them valuable. What are your preferences among the items?
action.label: context

action.content:
Thank you for being so cooperative! I appreciate the offer of the stamps - I do value them quite a bit actually, so that would work well for me.

As for my other preferences: I have some interest in the thimble but absolutely no interest in the soap. So if the stamps work for you to give up, and you have any interest in the soap or thimble, you should definitely take those. 

What do you value among the thimble and soap?
action.label: response

action.content:
Perfect! This works out really well. I value the soap highly and have some interest in the thimble as well. Since you value the stamps and I don't value them at all, and you don't value the soap while I do, we have great complementary preferences.

Based on what you've said, it sounds like the optimal division would be:
- You take: all 4 stamps
- I take: the soap and the thimble

Does this division work for you and maximize our combined score?
